text:
MEDICAL ONCOLOGY - FOLLOW-UP VISIT
Patient: Marcus R. Ellison
Diagnosis: Stage IV (M1c) BRAF V600E-mutant malignant melanoma, primary site: skin of upper back
Current Treatment: nivo 480 mg IV every 4 weeks (maintenance monotherapy, cycle 10)

---

CHIEF COMPLAINT

Mr. Ellison presents for his cycle 10 nivo infusion and scheduled disease reassessment. He reports feeling well overall and is pleased with his progress.

---

HISTORY OF PRESENT ILLNESS

Mr. Ellison is a 57-year-old White male with a diagnosis of stage IV, M1c, BRAF V600E-mutant nodular melanoma of the upper back, initially presenting at age 55 with extensive bilateral axillary and left cervical lymphadenopathy, multiple hepatic metastases (largest 3.1 cm), widespread cutaneous and subcutaneous satellite nodules on the trunk and extremities, and a right axillary conglomerate nodal mass measuring 4.8 cm. LDH at presentation was significantly elevated at 642 U/L. He was ECOG 1 at the time of metastatic diagnosis.

Given the high hepatic disease burden and markedly elevated LDH, a decision was made at multidisciplinary tumor board to initiate first-line targeted therapy with dabra (Tafinlar) 150 mg twice daily and Mekinist (Mekinist) 2 mg once daily for rapid disease control. He achieved an excellent early partial response -- palpable nodules regressed nearly completely by 6 weeks, and formal CT imaging at 3 months confirmed substantial shrinkage of hepatic and nodal target lesions, with LDH normalizing to the 160-180 range. Response subsequently stabilized at approximately 6 months. Progression was confirmed at approximately 8 months with significant re-growth of axillary and hepatic lesions, new hepatic foci, new subcutaneous deposits, and new right external iliac lymphadenopathy. LDH rebounded to 589 U/L. Acquired resistance biopsy and repeat molecular profiling demonstrated BRAF gene amplification, an emergent MEK1 p.P124S resistance mutation, and a new PIK3CA p.E545K mutation, consistent with the expected acquired resistance landscape for BRAF/MEK inhibitor therapy.

Tafinlar/tram was discontinued. After confirmatory brain MRI (negative for CNS involvement), he was transitioned to second-line Yervoy (Yervoy) 3 mg/kg IV plus nivo (Opdivo) 1 mg/kg IV every 3 weeks (CheckMate 067 dosing) with plans for 4 induction cycles followed by Opdivo maintenance. He completed cycles 1 and 2 with manageable grade 1-2 fatigue, pruritus, and mild rash, and early LDH improvement to 412 U/L. After cycle 3, he was hospitalized with grade 3 immune-mediated colitis: 8-10 watery bowel movements daily, severe abdominal cramping, and dehydration. Infectious workup was negative. Flexible sigmoidoscopy confirmed immune-mediated colitis with diffuse erythema, erosions, and ulcerations. He required IV methylprednisolone followed by an infliximab infusion for incomplete steroid response, with eventual complete mucosal healing confirmed on follow-up colonoscopy. ipi was permanently discontinued. He was eventually rechallenged with Opdivo as single-agent maintenance (480 mg IV every 4 weeks) after steroid taper to prednisone 10 mg/day and full GI symptom resolution.

Since resuming nivo maintenance approximately 6 months ago, Mr. Ellison has demonstrated an excellent and progressively deepening response. CT restaging at approximately 5 months after immunotherapy initiation showed a partial response with 53-57% reductions in target lesions. A subsequent CT at approximately 9 months after immunotherapy start showed continued deepening response, with the right axillary mass down to 0.9 cm, hepatic segment VII lesion at 0.6 cm, segment II lesion at 0.4 cm, and complete resolution of subcutaneous nodules. The overall reduction in target lesion burden from pre-immunotherapy baseline was approximately 77%. Brain MRI at the same timepoint confirmed no CNS involvement.

He developed grade 2 immune-mediated hypothyroidism approximately 8 months into immunotherapy (TSH 14.2 mIU/L, low free T4), with positive anti-TPO and anti-thyroglobulin antibodies. Levothyroxine was uptitrated to 75 mcg/day, with normalization of TSH to 3.2 mIU/L and resolution of symptoms. nivo was continued without interruption.

He now presents for cycle 10, approximately 10 months from immunotherapy initiation. He reports substantial improvement in his overall sense of well-being, no GI symptoms, and has returned to modified work as a landscaper on a light-duty basis. He remains on levothyroxine 75 mcg/day and low-dose prednisone 2.5 mg/day (tapering).

---

REVIEW OF SYSTEMS

Constitutional: Fatigue significantly improved compared to prior visits; he rates current energy at 7-8/10 compared to 3-4/10 at initiation of immunotherapy. No fever, no chills, no unintentional weight loss; in fact, he reports modest weight recovery of approximately 3 kg since worst period during colitis hospitalization.
Gastrointestinal: Bowel habits fully normalized. 1-2 well-formed stools per day. No diarrhea, no abdominal cramping, no blood per rectum. No nausea or vomiting. Appetite good.
Dermatologic: No new skin lesions detected by patient on self-exam. No rash, no pruritus at this time. Dermatology follow-up completed last month with no new cutaneous squamous cell carcinomas or other malignant lesions identified.
Endocrine: No symptoms of hypothyroidism recurrence. No cold intolerance, no constipation beyond baseline, no cognitive slowing. No symptoms suggesting adrenal insufficiency (no lightheadedness, no salt craving). No symptoms of hypophysitis.
Neurologic: No headaches, no visual changes, no focal neurologic symptoms.
Musculoskeletal: No new joint pains or myalgias. No symptoms concerning for immune arthritis.
Cardiovascular: No chest pain, no palpitations, no dyspnea on exertion.
Respiratory: No cough, no shortness of breath. Former smoker (quit 12 years ago), no respiratory complaints.
Genitourinary: No dysuria, no hematuria.
Hematologic: No unusual bruising or bleeding.

---

CURRENT MEDICATIONS

1. nivo (Opdivo) 480 mg IV every 4 weeks (cycle 10 today)
2. Levothyroxine (Synthroid) 75 mcg orally every morning, 30 minutes before food
3. Prednisone 2.5 mg orally daily (slow taper for prior immune-mediated colitis; on schedule to discontinue within 4 weeks)
4. Omeprazole 20 mg orally daily (GI prophylaxis while on steroids)
5. Acetaminophen 500-1000 mg orally PRN (for infusion-related discomfort, used rarely)

Allergies: NKDA

Vaccinations: Flu vaccine up to date. Pneumococcal vaccination given at time of immunotherapy initiation. COVID-19 vaccination current.

---

PHYSICAL EXAMINATION

Vital signs: Blood pressure 124/78 mmHg, heart rate 72 bpm (regular), respiratory rate 16/min, temperature 36.8 degrees C, SpO2 98% on room air. Weight: 81.2 kg. Height: 178 cm. BMI: 25.6 kg/m2.

General: Mr. Ellison is a well-appearing 57-year-old male in no acute distress. Alert and oriented x3. Well-nourished, well-groomed. Mood notably positive.

Head, Eyes, Ears, Nose, Throat: Sclerae non-icteric. Oral mucosa moist and intact. No mucositis. No lymphadenopathy in cervical region. Prior left cervical level II-III adenopathy is no longer clinically detectable.

Skin: Excision scar on right forearm from prior cuSCC resection, well-healed. No new suspicious pigmented or non-pigmented lesions on visible skin surfaces examined. No rash, no erythema, no palmar hyperkeratosis. Back: Primary melanoma excision site -- scar well-healed, no recurrence.

Lymph nodes: No palpable cervical, supraclavicular, infraclavicular, or axillary lymphadenopathy. Right axilla: area of prior conglomerate nodal disease -- on careful palpation, there is a small firm rubbery area that seems consistent with residual fibrotic/scar tissue measuring approximately 1 cm; no discrete discrete enlarging mass appreciated. This appears consistent with what was seen on most recent imaging. Left axilla: clear. Inguinal lymph nodes: not enlarged bilaterally.

Abdomen: Soft, non-tender, non-distended. No guarding or rebound. No organomegaly appreciated on palpation. No right upper quadrant tenderness. Bowel sounds normal.

Extremities: No edema. No new subcutaneous nodules palpated on arms, trunk, or legs. Prior satellite nodule sites (trunk, upper extremities) are no longer detectable on palpation.

Neurologic: Cranial nerves II-XII intact. Gait normal. No focal deficits.

Musculoskeletal: No joint effusions. Full range of motion in all major joints.

---

LABORATORY AND TEST RESULTS

Complete blood count:
- WBC 6.1 x10^9/L (normal differential)
- Hemoglobin 13.1 g/dL (improved from 11.4 g/dL at 6-month assessment; normalized)
- Platelets 221 x10^9/L

Comprehensive metabolic panel:
- Sodium 138 mEq/L, Potassium 4.1 mEq/L, Bicarbonate 24 mEq/L, Creatinine 0.9 mg/dL (eGFR >60)
- Total bilirubin 0.7 mg/dL
- AST 28 U/L, ALT 31 U/L, Alkaline phosphatase 74 U/L (all within normal limits; notable improvement from AST 94/ALT 78 at immunotherapy initiation)
- Albumin 4.2 g/dL

LDH: 148 U/L (within normal range; down from 642 U/L at initial presentation and 589 U/L at progression)

Thyroid function:
- TSH 2.8 mIU/L (within normal range on levothyroxine 75 mcg/day)
- Free T4: 1.1 ng/dL (normal)

Morning cortisol: 14.2 mcg/dL (adequate; no adrenal insufficiency; patient is on low-dose prednisone 2.5 mg/day)

Urinalysis: No proteinuria, no hematuria (baseline renal immune monitoring).

---

IMAGING RESULTS

Most recent CT chest/abdomen/pelvis (approximately 1 month prior, 9-month immunotherapy restaging): Continued deepening partial response compared to pre-immunotherapy baseline. Right axillary residual soft tissue 0.9 cm (from 3.4 cm at progression). Hepatic segment VII lesion 0.6 cm (from 2.8 cm). Hepatic segment II lesion 0.4 cm (from 1.6 cm). Segment IV and VI hepatic lesions resolved. No subcutaneous nodules. Right external iliac node 0.4 cm (normal by size). No new lesions identified. Overall approximately 77% reduction in target lesion burden from pre-immunotherapy baseline. LDH 152 U/L at time of that scan.

Most recent brain MRI (same timepoint, 9 months into immunotherapy): Negative for intracranial metastases or leptomeningeal disease. Normal study.

Patient was asked about PET-CT consideration raised at last visit. He is agreeable and we are scheduling this for comprehensive metabolic response assessment.

---

ASSESSMENT AND PLAN

1. Stage IV BRAF V600E-mutant metastatic melanoma -- DURABLE PARTIAL RESPONSE on Opdivo monotherapy maintenance (cycle 10)

Mr. Ellison is doing remarkably well. After a complicated treatment course -- first-line Tafinlar/Mekinist with excellent initial response and progression at 8 months, followed by second-line Yervoy/Opdivo induction complicated by grade 3 immune-mediated colitis requiring infliximab, and subsequent transition to nivo monotherapy maintenance -- he has now achieved and maintained a deep partial response. His hepatic metastases have regressed dramatically, subcutaneous deposits have fully resolved, and the right axillary residual tissue appears to be fibrotic scar rather than active disease. LDH has been stable in the normal range for several months, hemoglobin has normalized, and liver function tests have returned to entirely normal levels.

From a prognostic perspective, this is an encouraging trajectory. While he did not complete the full 4 cycles of ipi/nivo (stopping after 3 due to grade 3 colitis), the immunotherapy induction was sufficient to generate a durable immune response that has been sustained on Opdivo monotherapy. Patients achieving this depth of response on PD-1-based therapy in the metastatic melanoma setting have approximately 20-30% probability of durable long-term remission. The near-normalization of his LDH and the progressive deepening of response on serial imaging are favorable signs.

Plan:
- Administer Opdivo 480 mg IV today (cycle 10). Pre-medicate per institutional protocol (diphenhydramine 25 mg IV, acetaminophen 650 mg orally). Monitor for infusion-related reactions.
- Schedule PET-CT (18F-FDG whole body with brain) for comprehensive metabolic response assessment. This will provide a more complete picture of disease activity and help guide future discussions about treatment duration.
- Continue current nivo maintenance schedule. No basis to discontinue at this time given M1c disease at presentation, high initial volume, and ongoing (albeit deepening) residual imaging findings.
- Next CT restaging to be performed at 4-month intervals unless clinical change.

2. Immune-mediated hypothyroidism (grade 2, on Opdivo) -- STABLE, WELL-CONTROLLED

TSH 2.8 mIU/L and free T4 1.1 ng/dL on levothyroxine 75 mcg/day -- excellent thyroid hormone replacement. Patient denies any symptoms of hypothyroidism. This is likely a permanent condition requiring lifelong thyroid hormone replacement. No change to levothyroxine dose at this time.
- Continue levothyroxine 75 mcg/day
- Repeat TSH and free T4 in 8-10 weeks
- Endocrinology follow-up as needed

3. Prior grade 3 immune-mediated colitis (Yervoy/nivo) -- RESOLVED, IN REMISSION

Patient remains entirely asymptomatic from a GI standpoint. Bowel function is completely normal. He is down to prednisone 2.5 mg/day and on track to discontinue within the next 4 weeks. Omeprazole to be discontinued shortly after steroid discontinuation unless he develops GI symptoms.
- Taper prednisone to 2.5 mg/day for 2 more weeks, then discontinue
- Discontinue omeprazole 2-4 weeks after prednisone cessation
- Surveillance colonoscopy deferred; patient asymptomatic and prior colitis fully healed on 10-week follow-up scope
- Low threshold for GI evaluation if any symptoms emerge
- Yervoy permanently discontinued (grade 3 irAE)

4. Cutaneous surveillance (prior cuSCC, BRAF inhibitor history)

Monthly dermatology surveillance was initiated at time of cuSCC diagnosis. Most recent dermatology visit (last month) was unremarkable. No new suspicious lesions.
- Continue annual comprehensive dermatologic skin checks
- Patient educated on self-skin exam monthly
- No dose modification of current immunotherapy required for this issue

5. Performance status and quality of life

ECOG 0. Patient has returned to work (light-duty landscaping). He appears in excellent spirits and has demonstrated strong adherence to all treatment components and monitoring requirements. Psychosocial support services discussed; patient declined at this time, feels well-supported by family.

6. Follow-up

Return to clinic in 4 weeks for cycle 11 nivo. PET-CT to be completed prior to that visit if scheduling permits. Thyroid function tests in 8-10 weeks. Brain MRI surveillance scheduled in approximately 3 months (at 13 months from immunotherapy initiation). Ongoing dermatology follow-up per their schedule.

Electronically signed,
Attending Medical Oncologist, Melanoma/Skin Cancer Program
